key_insight: The paper derives a predictive law showing that LLM ensemble lift can be explained by decomposing diversity into rescue and damage mass, with accuracy-adjusted correlation and accuracy gap being strong predictors of when ensembles improve.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a compact way to forecast whether combining multiple LLMs will outperform a single model, helping design more reliable voting and routing systems.
method: Derived an exact lift decomposition into rescue and damage masses.
Defined c6_adj, accuracy gap, and collective accuracy as pre-pooling predictors.
Validated a frozen heuristic across multiple benchmarks and model pairs.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on 767,520 inferences from ten open-weight models across SuperGPQA, GPQA Diamond, and a forensic multi-turn tool-use benchmark, with strong transfer performance and high agreement between swap mass and realized lift.
watch_item: The calibration was tuned on one dataset and vote split, so performance may depend on benchmark mix, model family, and whether the ensemble setting matches the tested conditions.
paper_title: Quantifying Diversity of Thought: A Predictive Law of Weighted LLM Ensemble Lift
